essence: Fuses metal with heat, precision, and technical mastery to build structures that last.

identity.title: Welder
identity.summary: Joins, cuts, and shapes metal components using welding equipment, working across construction, manufacturing, engineering, and repair industries.

salary.notes: Specialist welding (TIG, underwater, aerospace, high‑precision fabrication) can exceed £50k–£80k+. Hazard pay and overtime often increase earnings.

qualifications: NVQ or Diploma in Welding and Fabrication
Certification in MIG, TIG, or Arc Welding
Strong Understanding of Safety Procedures and Metal Properties

lifestyle.description: Welders work in hands‑on, technical environments where precision, safety, and steady focus are essential. Days involve preparing materials, setting up equipment, reading technical drawings, and performing welds that must meet strict structural or aesthetic standards. Work may take place in workshops, construction sites, factories, or shipyards, often requiring protective gear and adherence to safety protocols. The role can be physically demanding and sometimes involves heat, noise, or confined spaces. Yet the satisfaction is high — welders create strong, lasting structures and components. This lifestyle suits individuals who enjoy practical craftsmanship, technical skill, and seeing tangible results from their work.

habits: Checks equipment and safety gear carefully
Practises steady hand control and precision
Inspects joints for strength and quality
